title: RPL modules use .expect() for state machine transitions
description:
Several RPL modules use .expect() for state transitions:

**lichen-rpl/src/trickle.rs:97:**
```rust
.expect("stopped or active Trickle timer can begin an interval");
```

**lichen-rpl/src/dodag.rs:281,306,340:**
```rust
.expect("DODAG version adoption cannot demote a root");
.expect("joined DODAG can return to unjoined");
.expect("non-root DODAG can join through a selected parent");
```

**lichen-rpl/src/routing.rs:184:**
```rust
.expect("fresh or stale route entry can refresh");
```

These encode state machine invariants as expects. Consider:
1. Using debug_assert! if these are programmer-error checks
2. Returning Result if callers should handle violations
3. Making impossible states unrepresentable via type design
